# Link Balance Rows to Their Generating Transaction — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** On the Accounts page, a balance-history row that was generated by a transaction shows a receipt icon that opens that transaction's details, read-only, in a modal.

**Architecture:** Resolve the (one-to-one) `txn → account_balance` link with a `LEFT JOIN` so each `AccountBalance` carries an optional `linkedTransactionId`. The Accounts page renders a receipt button on linked rows; clicking it lazily fetches the full transaction via a new `get_transaction` command and shows it in a read-only `TransactionDetail` modal.

**Tech Stack:** Rust (Tauri commands, libsql), ts-rs (type generation), Vue 3 + `<script setup>`, Pinia, Nuxt UI (`UModal`, `UButton`, `i-lucide-*`).

**Conventions observed in this repo:**
- Backend logic is tested via integration tests in `src-tauri/tests/*.rs` (in-memory libsql). Run with `cargo test` from `src-tauri/`.
- ts-rs regenerates `src/lib/types/*.ts` as a side effect of `cargo test`.
- Frontend tests cover **pure logic only** (`src/lib/**/*.test.ts`); Vue components are verified by `vue-tsc` (`npm run build`) and manual run, not component tests. This plan follows that — no component unit tests.

---

## File Structure

| File | Responsibility | Change |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `src-tauri/src/models.rs` | Shared serialized models | Add `linked_transaction_id` to `AccountBalance` |
| `src-tauri/src/commands/accounts.rs` | Account/balance queries | LEFT JOIN in both balance queries; read new column |
| `src-tauri/src/commands/transactions.rs` | Transaction queries | `get_transaction` inner fn + `get_transaction_cmd` |
| `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` | Tauri command registry | Register `get_transaction_cmd` |
| `src-tauri/tests/transactions.rs` | Backend integration tests | New tests for link resolution + `get_transaction` |
| `src/lib/types/AccountBalance.ts` | Generated type | Regenerated by ts-rs (do not hand-edit) |
| `src/lib/api/transactions.ts` | Frontend command bindings | Add `getTransaction(id)` |
| `src/components/TransactionDetail.vue` | Read-only transaction view | New presentational component |
| `src/components/BalanceRow.vue` | Balance-history table row | Receipt button + `view-transaction` emit |
| `src/pages/Accounts.vue` | Accounts page | Modal + lazy-fetch wiring |

## Task 1: Add `linked_transaction_id` to the balance model and resolve it in queries

**Files:**
- Modify: `src-tauri/src/models.rs:34-39` (`AccountBalance` struct)
- Modify: `src-tauri/src/commands/accounts.rs` (`row_to_balance`, `list_account_balances`, `list_all_balances`)
- Test: `src-tauri/tests/transactions.rs`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing tests**

Append to `src-tauri/tests/transactions.rs`:

```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn balances_expose_linked_transaction_id() {
    let conn = setup().await;
    let acct = accounts::create_account(&conn, &NewAccount {
        name: "Checking".into(), r#type: "checking".into(), institution: None,
        include_in_fire_calculations: false, created_at: "2026-01-01".into() }).await.unwrap();

    // A manually-entered balance has no linking transaction.
    accounts::add_balance(&conn, &accounts::NewBalance {
        account_id: acct, balance: 1000.0, recorded_at: "2026-02-01".into() }).await.unwrap();

    // A transaction with the balance switch ON generates a linked snapshot (1000 - 40 = 960).
    let mut t = new_txn(acct, 40.0, "expense");
    t.update_balance = true;
    let txn_id = transactions::create_transaction(&conn, &t).await.unwrap();

    let balances = accounts::list_all_balances(&conn).await.unwrap();
    let manual = balances.iter().find(|b| b.balance == 1000.0).unwrap();
    let generated = balances.iter().find(|b| b.balance == 960.0).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(manual.linked_transaction_id, None);
    assert_eq!(generated.linked_transaction_id, Some(txn_id));
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn transfer_balances_link_to_same_transaction() {
    let conn = setup().await;
    let a = accounts::create_account(&conn, &NewAccount {
        name: "A".into(), r#type: "checking".into(), institution: None,
        include_in_fire_calculations: false, created_at: "2026-01-01".into() }).await.unwrap();
    let b = accounts::create_account(&conn, &NewAccount {
        name: "B".into(), r#type: "savings".into(), institution: None,
        include_in_fire_calculations: false, created_at: "2026-01-01".into() }).await.unwrap();
    accounts::add_balance(&conn, &accounts::NewBalance {
        account_id: a, balance: 1000.0, recorded_at: "2026-02-01".into() }).await.unwrap();
    accounts::add_balance(&conn, &accounts::NewBalance {
        account_id: b, balance: 200.0, recorded_at: "2026-02-01".into() }).await.unwrap();

    let mut t = new_txn(a, 300.0, "transfer");
    t.transfer_account_id = Some(b);
    t.update_balance = true;
    let txn_id = transactions::create_transaction(&conn, &t).await.unwrap();

    let balances = accounts::list_all_balances(&conn).await.unwrap();
    // Source snapshot: 1000 - 300 = 700; destination: 200 + 300 = 500.
    let src = balances.iter().find(|x| x.account_id == a && x.balance == 700.0).unwrap();
    let dst = balances.iter().find(|x| x.account_id == b && x.balance == 500.0).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(src.linked_transaction_id, Some(txn_id));
    assert_eq!(dst.linked_transaction_id, Some(txn_id));
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the tests to verify they fail**

Run: `cd src-tauri && cargo test --test transactions balances_expose_linked_transaction_id transfer_balances_link_to_same_transaction`
Expected: compile error — `AccountBalance` has no field `linked_transaction_id`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Add the model field**

In `src-tauri/src/models.rs`, change the `AccountBalance` struct to:

```rust
#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, TS, Clone)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
#[ts(export, export_to = "../../src/lib/types/")]
pub struct AccountBalance {
    pub id: i32,
    pub account_id: i32,
    pub balance: f64,
    pub recorded_at: String,
    pub linked_transaction_id: Option<i32>,
}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Populate it in `row_to_balance`**

In `src-tauri/src/commands/accounts.rs`, change `row_to_balance` to read the new fifth column:

```rust
fn row_to_balance(row: &libsql::Row) -> Result<AccountBalance, String> {
    Ok(AccountBalance {
        id: row.get(0).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?,
        account_id: row.get(1).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?,
        balance: row.get(2).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?,
        recorded_at: row.get(3).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?,
        linked_transaction_id: row.get(4).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?,
    })
}
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Add the LEFT JOIN to both balance queries**

In `src-tauri/src/commands/accounts.rs`, change `list_account_balances`'s query to:

```rust
            "SELECT b.id, b.account_id, b.balance, b.recorded_at, t.id \
             FROM account_balance b \
             LEFT JOIN txn t \
               ON t.generated_balance_id = b.id OR t.generated_balance_to_id = b.id \
             WHERE b.account_id = ?1 ORDER BY b.recorded_at",
```

And change `list_all_balances`'s query to:

```rust
            "SELECT b.id, b.account_id, b.balance, b.recorded_at, t.id \
             FROM account_balance b \
             LEFT JOIN txn t \
               ON t.generated_balance_id = b.id OR t.generated_balance_to_id = b.id \
             ORDER BY b.recorded_at",
```

(The link is one-to-one, so the join never multiplies balance rows.)

- [ ] **Step 6: Run the tests to verify they pass (and the whole suite stays green)**

Run: `cd src-tauri && cargo test`
Expected: PASS, including the two new tests and all existing ones. This run also regenerates `src/lib/types/AccountBalance.ts` with the new `linkedTransactionId: number | null` field.

- [ ] **Step 7: Confirm the type regenerated**

Run: `grep linkedTransactionId src/lib/types/AccountBalance.ts`
Expected: a line containing `linkedTransactionId: number | null`.

- [ ] **Step 8: Commit**

```bash
git add src-tauri/src/models.rs src-tauri/src/commands/accounts.rs src-tauri/tests/transactions.rs src/lib/types/AccountBalance.ts
git commit -m "feat: resolve linked transaction id on balance snapshots

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

## Task 2: Add a `get_transaction` command

**Files:**
- Modify: `src-tauri/src/commands/transactions.rs` (new inner fn + command wrapper)
- Modify: `src-tauri/src/lib.rs:37` (register command)
- Test: `src-tauri/tests/transactions.rs`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing tests**

Append to `src-tauri/tests/transactions.rs`:

```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn get_transaction_returns_row() {
    let conn = setup().await;
    let acct = accounts::create_account(&conn, &NewAccount {
        name: "Checking".into(), r#type: "checking".into(), institution: None,
        include_in_fire_calculations: false, created_at: "2026-01-01".into() }).await.unwrap();
    let id = transactions::create_transaction(&conn, &new_txn(acct, 1000.0, "income"))
        .await.unwrap();

    let txn = transactions::get_transaction(&conn, id).await.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(txn.id, id);
    assert_eq!(txn.amount, 1000.0);
    assert_eq!(txn.r#type, "income");
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn get_transaction_missing_id_errors() {
    let conn = setup().await;
    let result = transactions::get_transaction(&conn, 9999).await;
    assert!(result.is_err());
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the tests to verify they fail**

Run: `cd src-tauri && cargo test --test transactions get_transaction_returns_row get_transaction_missing_id_errors`
Expected: compile error — no function `get_transaction` in `transactions`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement `get_transaction`**

In `src-tauri/src/commands/transactions.rs`, add after `list_transactions` (it reuses the existing `COLS` const and `row_to_txn` helper):

```rust
pub async fn get_transaction(conn: &Connection, id: i32) -> Result<Transaction, String> {
    let sql = format!("SELECT {COLS} FROM txn WHERE id = ?1");
    let mut rows = conn
        .query(&sql, params![id])
        .await
        .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
    match rows.next().await.map_err(|e| e.to_string())? {
        Some(row) => row_to_txn(&row),
        None => Err(format!("transaction {id} not found")),
    }
}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Add the command wrapper**

In `src-tauri/src/commands/transactions.rs`, add alongside the other `#[tauri::command]` wrappers:

```rust
#[tauri::command]
pub async fn get_transaction_cmd(db: State<'_, Db>, id: i32) -> Result<Transaction, String> {
    let conn = db.conn().await?;
    get_transaction(&conn, id).await
}
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Register the command**

In `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, inside `tauri::generate_handler![ ... ]`, add after `commands::transactions::list_transactions_cmd,`:

```rust
            commands::transactions::get_transaction_cmd,
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Run the tests to verify they pass**

Run: `cd src-tauri && cargo test`
Expected: PASS, including the two new tests.

- [ ] **Step 7: Commit**

```bash
git add src-tauri/src/commands/transactions.rs src-tauri/src/lib.rs src-tauri/tests/transactions.rs
git commit -m "feat: add get_transaction command

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

## Task 3: Add the `getTransaction` frontend binding

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/lib/api/transactions.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the binding**

In `src/lib/api/transactions.ts`, add an import for the `Transaction` type at the top (next to the existing `TransactionPage` import):

```ts
import type { Transaction } from '../types/Transaction'
```

Then add this export alongside the other command bindings:

```ts
export const getTransaction = (id: number) =>
  invoke<Transaction>('get_transaction_cmd', { id })
```

## Task 4: Create the read-only `TransactionDetail` component

**Files:**
- Create: `src/components/TransactionDetail.vue`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the component**

Create `src/components/TransactionDetail.vue`:

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
import { useAccountsStore } from '../stores/accounts'
import type { Transaction } from '../lib/types/Transaction'

defineProps<{ transaction: Transaction }>()

const accounts = useAccountsStore()

function accountName(id: number | null): string {
  if (id == null) return '—'
  return accounts.accounts.find((a) => a.id === id)?.name ?? `#${id}`
}

const money = (n: number) =>
  n.toLocaleString('en-US', { style: 'currency', currency: 'USD' })
</script>

<template>
  <dl class="grid grid-cols-3 gap-x-4 gap-y-3 text-sm">
    <dt class="text-gray-500">Date</dt>
    <dd class="col-span-2">{{ transaction.date }}</dd>

    <dt class="text-gray-500">Description</dt>
    <dd class="col-span-2">{{ transaction.description }}</dd>

    <dt class="text-gray-500">Amount</dt>
    <dd
      class="col-span-2 tabular-nums font-medium"
      :class="transaction.type === 'income' ? 'text-green-600' : transaction.type === 'expense' ? 'text-red-600' : ''"
    >
      {{ money(transaction.amount) }}
    </dd>

    <dt class="text-gray-500">Type</dt>
    <dd class="col-span-2 capitalize">{{ transaction.type }}</dd>

    <dt class="text-gray-500">Category</dt>
    <dd class="col-span-2">{{ transaction.category }}</dd>

    <dt class="text-gray-500">Account</dt>
    <dd class="col-span-2">
      {{ accountName(transaction.accountId) }}
      <span v-if="transaction.type === 'transfer'"> → {{ accountName(transaction.transferAccountId) }}</span>
    </dd>

    <template v-if="transaction.isContribution">
      <dt class="text-gray-500">Contribution</dt>
      <dd class="col-span-2">Yes</dd>
    </template>

    <dt class="text-gray-500">Source</dt>
    <dd class="col-span-2">{{ transaction.importSource }}</dd>
  </dl>
</template>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Typecheck**

Run: `npm run build`
Expected: `vue-tsc` passes (the component is unused so far, but must compile).

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add src/components/TransactionDetail.vue
git commit -m "feat: read-only TransactionDetail component

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

## Task 5: Add the receipt button + emit to `BalanceRow`

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/components/BalanceRow.vue`

- [ ] **Step 1: Declare the emit**

In `src/components/BalanceRow.vue`, just after the existing `const props = defineProps<{ balance: AccountBalance }>()` line, add:

```ts
const emit = defineEmits<{ (e: 'view-transaction', id: number): void }>()
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Render the receipt button on linked rows**

In `src/components/BalanceRow.vue`, in the `<template v-else>` actions cell, add the receipt button as the first child of the `<div class="flex justify-end gap-1">`, before the Edit button:

```vue
        <div class="flex justify-end gap-1">
          <UButton
            v-if="balance.linkedTransactionId != null"
            size="xs"
            variant="ghost"
            icon="i-lucide-receipt"
            :title="'View linked transaction'"
            @click="emit('view-transaction', balance.linkedTransactionId)"
          />
          <UButton size="xs" variant="ghost" @click="startEdit">Edit</UButton>
          <UButton size="xs" variant="ghost" color="error" @click="remove">Delete</UButton>
        </div>
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Typecheck**

Run: `npm run build`
Expected: `vue-tsc` passes. (If it complains that `linkedTransactionId` could be `null` inside the click handler, note the `v-if` guard narrows it — Vue template type-narrowing handles this; if a stricter error appears, change the handler to `@click="balance.linkedTransactionId != null && emit('view-transaction', balance.linkedTransactionId)"`.)

## Task 6: Wire the modal into the Accounts page

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/pages/Accounts.vue`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add imports, state, and handler**

In `src/pages/Accounts.vue` `<script setup>`:

Add these imports near the other component/type imports:

```ts
import TransactionDetail from '../components/TransactionDetail.vue'
import type { Transaction } from '../lib/types/Transaction'
import { getTransaction } from '../lib/api/transactions'
```

Add this state and handler (e.g. after the `editingAccount` ref / `openEdit` block):

```ts
const isTransactionModalOpen = ref(false)
const viewingTransaction = ref<Transaction | null>(null)

async function openTransaction(id: number) {
  viewingTransaction.value = await getTransaction(id)
  isTransactionModalOpen.value = true
}

watch(isTransactionModalOpen, (open) => {
  if (!open) viewingTransaction.value = null
})
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Listen for the emit on `BalanceRow`**

In `src/pages/Accounts.vue`, change the `<BalanceRow ... />` usage to add the listener:

```vue
                  <BalanceRow
                    v-for="b in accountBalances(account.id)"
                    :key="b.id"
                    :balance="b"
                    @view-transaction="openTransaction"
                  />
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Add the detail modal**

In `src/pages/Accounts.vue`, add this `UModal` just before the closing `</div>` of the root `<div class="p-6 max-w-4xl">` (e.g. right after the Archived section block):

```vue
    <UModal v-model:open="isTransactionModalOpen" title="Transaction details">
      <template #body>
        <TransactionDetail v-if="viewingTransaction" :transaction="viewingTransaction" />
      </template>
    </UModal>
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Typecheck**

Run: `npm run build`
Expected: `vue-tsc` passes, build completes.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add src/pages/Accounts.vue
git commit -m "feat: open linked transaction details from Accounts balance history

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

## Task 7: Full verification

- [ ] **Step 1: Backend test suite**

Run: `cd src-tauri && cargo test`
Expected: all tests pass.

- [ ] **Step 2: Frontend logic tests**

Run: `npm test`
Expected: all `src/lib/**/*.test.ts` pass (no regressions).

- [ ] **Step 3: Typecheck / build**

Run: `npm run build`
Expected: `vue-tsc --noEmit` clean, `vite build` succeeds.

- [ ] **Step 4: Manual smoke test (in the running app)**

Run: `npm run tauri dev`
Verify:
1. On the Accounts page, add a transaction (with the balance switch ON) on the Transactions page for an account, then return to Accounts — the corresponding Balance History row shows a receipt icon.
2. A manually-added balance snapshot shows **no** receipt icon.
3. Clicking the receipt icon opens the "Transaction details" modal with the correct read-only fields.
4. For a transfer, both the source and destination accounts' generated rows show the receipt icon, and both open the same transfer transaction.

---

## Self-Review Notes

- **Spec coverage:** Data-layer join (Task 1), lazy `get_transaction` fetch (Tasks 2–3), `TransactionDetail` read-only modal (Tasks 4 & 6), receipt-icon affordance on linked rows only (Task 5), transfer both-sides linkage (tested in Task 1, verified in Task 7). "Open in Transactions" button intentionally out of scope.
- **Type consistency:** `linked_transaction_id` (Rust) → `linkedTransactionId` (camelCase via serde/ts-rs) used consistently in `BalanceRow.vue` and the join. `get_transaction`/`get_transaction_cmd`/`getTransaction` names align across layers. Event name `view-transaction` matches between `BalanceRow` emit and `Accounts.vue` listener.
- **No placeholders:** every code step shows complete code.
